sourceMovie.title: Hero
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hero works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Red Cliff
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hero clicked for you, Red Cliff (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hero clicked for you, Red Cliff II (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hero?
faq.answer: Try Red Cliff, Red Cliff II, Braveheart—each shares DNA with Hero while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hero?
faq.answer: Hero (2002) is best described as Drama, Adventure, Action, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hero worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
